title: Abutilon cistiflorum
taxonomy: Abutilon cistiflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Abutilon and is a part of the Malvales order. The plant is native to South America and is also known as the rock rose or clock flower. Its classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its five-petaled flowers and hairy leaves. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Abutilon cistiflorum is closely related to other plants like hibiscus and okra.
uses: Abutilon cistiflorum is a plant species with various traditional uses, particularly in folk medicine. In some regions, it is used to treat gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. The plant's leaves and roots are often employed to create remedies for fever reduction and to ease inflammation. Additionally, it is believed to possess antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating infections. More research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential risks associated with using Abutilon cistiflorum for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Abutilon cistiflorum contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. These compounds are responsible for its potential medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. preparations: Abutilon cistiflorum is not commonly referenced in mainstream herbal literature. intro: Abutilon cistiflorum, also known as the cistiflorum cotton bush or abutilon, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its purported health benefits in reducing pain and improving digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon cistiflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and may also be used in the form of a tincture or topical cream for localized treatment.
